name: Shield of Faith
description: The Shield of Faith, as described in Ephesians 6:16, is not a physical object but a spiritual one. It represents unwavering belief and trust in God and His promises. This shield is wielded by believers to extinguish the fiery darts of the evil one, which symbolize temptations, doubts, and attacks against their faith. The Shield of Faith is essential for spiritual warfare, providing protection against the forces of darkness. It is strengthened through prayer, scripture reading, and fellowship with other believers. Without faith, a believer is vulnerable to spiritual harm. The shield is not a passive defense but an active engagement with God's power. It requires conscious effort to maintain and strengthen one's faith. The more fortified the shield, the more resilient the believer becomes against spiritual attacks. It represents the ultimate reliance on God's grace and protection. The strength of the shield is directly proportional to the depth of one's faith. The Bible emphasizes the importance of faith as a gift from God, and the Shield of Faith symbolizes the constant exercise and nurturing of that gift. It's a testament to the power of belief in overcoming adversity and spiritual challenges, serving as a constant reminder of God's unwavering support.
subtitle: Spiritual defense against evil.

keyMyths.title: The Armor of God
keyMyths.description: Ephesians 6 describes the armor of God, including the Shield of Faith, as essential for spiritual warfare against evil forces. It is a call to believers to actively defend their faith.

keyMyths.title: Abraham's Faith
keyMyths.description: Abraham's unwavering faith in God's promise, even when it seemed impossible, serves as an example of the strength and power of faith. It reinforces the idea that faith can overcome any obstacle.

sources.author: Paul the Apostle
sources.description: Describes the armor of God, including the Shield of Faith.
sources.text: Describes the armor of God, including the Shield of Faith.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: Contains numerous accounts of faith and its power.
